meta.title: StrongTypes | kalandra.tech — Safer C# Types
meta.description: StrongTypes is a small C# library for safer, more expressive code: NonEmptyString, validated numerics, NonEmptyEnumerable, Maybe<T>, and Result<T, TError>.

hero.titlePrefix: A few small types that make C#
hero.description: StrongTypes adds focused, validated wrapper types that move invariants into the type system — "a string that is never empty" or "an integer that is always positive". Validation happens at the boundary, so invalid data never reaches your handlers.
hero.copyFailed: Could not copy. Please copy manually.

why.title: Why bother with strong types?
why.subtitle: Most C# code defends against invalid input by re-validating it everywhere. StrongTypes flips that around: validate once, at the boundary, and let the compiler enforce the rest.

points.title: Invariants in the type
points.description: If a method takes NonEmptyString, it cannot receive an empty one. The compiler — not a runtime guard clause — does the work.

points.title: Validation at the edge
points.description: Built-in System.Text.Json converters reject invalid payloads during deserialization, before your endpoint method is even called.

points.title: Expressive intent
points.description: A signature like Result<Order, OrderError> Place(Positive<int> qty) tells the reader exactly what's allowed and what can fail.

points.title: Small and focused
points.description: No heavy framework, no runtime reflection magic. Drop it in alongside your existing code — types compose with what you already have.

items.name: NonEmptyEnumerable<T>
items.description: Read-only sequence with at least one element — Head and Tail are always safe to access.

items.name: Maybe<T>
items.description: Optional values with Map and FlatMap. Useful for HTTP PATCH (don't touch / clear / set).

items.name: Result<T, TError>
items.description: Explicit failure handling without exceptions. Includes async support.

items.name: FsCheck generators
items.description: Via the Kalicz.StrongTypes.FsCheck companion package for property-based testing.

examples.title: How it looks
examples.subtitle: A few snippets to give you the feel of it. Full docs and more examples are on GitHub.

items.title: Create a NonEmptyString
items.description: Three flavours: throw, try, or fluent extension. Pick whichever suits the call site.

items.title: Validate at the API boundary
items.description: The JSON converter rejects invalid input during deserialization. Your endpoint never sees a bad value.

items.title: Compose with Maybe<T>
items.description: Chain optional operations without nested null checks.

items.title: Explicit failures with Result<T, TError>
items.description: Pattern-match on Success or Error — no exceptions, no out parameters.

items.title: Three-state PATCH with Maybe<T>
items.description: Distinguish "don't touch", "clear to null", and "set to value" — a long-standing pain point in REST APIs.

items.title: GitHub repository
items.description: Source, README, releases, and issue tracker.

items.title: NuGet — Core
items.description: The main package. Contains all validated and algebraic types.

items.title: NuGet — EF Core
items.description: Value converters for persisting StrongTypes with Entity Framework Core.

items.title: NuGet — FsCheck
items.description: Generators for property-based testing of StrongTypes values.
